person_name: Prusias II of Bithynia (king, r. 182–149 BCE)
person_name_full: Prusias II of Bithynia (king, r. 182–149 BCE)
notes: King of Bithynia, reigned 182–149 BCE, described as irritable and involved in political affairs with Rome.

docs.title: Roman History, Book Book 21
docs.author: Cassius Dio
docs.excerpt: This monarch, being old and of an irritable disposition, became possessed by a fear that the Bithynians would expel him from his kingdom, choosing in his stead his son Nicomedes. So he sent him to Rome on some pretext, with orders to make that his home.
